gaps.snippet: # [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Terminate the process with `code`. Does not return. The exit status is the caller's"] # [doc = " explicit choice — never a silent `0` substituted for an error path (G2): a program that wants to"] # [doc = " signal failure passes a non-zero `code`."] pub fn exit (code : i32) -> ! { std :: process :: exit (code) }
gaps.reason: signed integer `i32` — Binary{N} is documented unsigned-magnitude (lib/std/cmp.myc); mapping a signed type onto it would misrepresent twos-complement semantics, so this is left an explicit gap rather than guessed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " \\[Declared\\] Read environment variable `name`. Returns `None` if the variable is unset **or** is"] # [doc = " not valid Unicode — an explicit absence, never an empty-string stand-in (G2). Use when a missing"] # [doc = " variable is a recoverable condition; the `None` must be handled, not assumed present."] # [must_use] pub fn get_env (name : & str) -> Option < String > { std :: env :: var (name) . ok () }

# [doc = " \\[Declared\\] A command-line argument that was not valid UTF-8, reported by its **position**"] # [doc = " (`index` — arg 0 is the program name) so the offending input is named, never silently dropped"] # [doc = " (G2). Carries the lossy [`String::from_utf8_lossy`] rendering (`lossy`) so an `EXPLAIN`/diagnostic"] # [doc = " can *show* the bad arg without fabricating a faithful round-trip it cannot deliver."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct NonUtf8Arg { # [doc = " 0-based position of the offending argument (0 = program name)."] pub index : usize , # [doc = " `String::from_utf8_lossy` rendering of the raw argument (U+FFFD for each invalid byte)."] pub lossy : String , }
gaps.reason: struct `NonUtf8Arg` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: impl std :: fmt :: Display for NonUtf8Arg { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut std :: fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> std :: fmt :: Result { write ! (f , "command-line argument at index {} is not valid UTF-8 (lossy: {:?})" , self . index , self . lossy) } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `fmt` signature: unsupported Rust type form `& mut std :: fmt :: Formatter < '_ >`

gaps.snippet: impl std :: error :: Error for NonUtf8Arg { }

# [doc = " \\[Declared\\] The process's command-line arguments (including arg 0), parsed to `String`s. Built on"] # [doc = " `args_os`: a non-UTF-8 argument is an explicit `Err(NonUtf8Arg)` naming the offending **index** —"] # [doc = " never a silently-dropped element (the previous `filter_map` behaviour) that would shift every"] # [doc = " following arg's position, and (unlike `std::env::args()`) never a *panic* (G2). The first"] # [doc = " non-UTF-8 arg short-circuits; callers needing the faithful, position-stable raw form should use"] # [doc = " `std::env::args_os` directly (this floor is the validated-UTF-8 convenience)."] pub fn args () -> Result < Vec < String > , NonUtf8Arg > { parse_args (std :: env :: args_os ()) }
gaps.reason: generic type path `Result < Vec < String > , NonUtf8Arg >` — type-argument mapping not confirmed

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Core parse step factored over an iterator of [`OsString`](std::ffi::OsString) so it is"] # [doc = " unit-testable without real `argv`. Maps each arg through `into_string`; the first non-UTF-8 arg"] # [doc = " becomes an `Err(NonUtf8Arg)` naming its index (never-silent, G2) rather than being dropped."] pub (crate) fn parse_args (raw : impl IntoIterator < Item = std :: ffi :: OsString > ,) -> Result < Vec < String > , NonUtf8Arg > { raw . into_iter () . enumerate () . map (| (index , a) | { a . into_string () . map_err (| bad | NonUtf8Arg { index , lossy : bad . to_string_lossy () . into_owned () , }) }) . collect () }
gaps.reason: unsupported Rust type form `impl IntoIterator < Item = std :: ffi :: OsString >`
